title: Real World VoiceEQ tests whether voice AI feels human in real conversations
friendlyTitle: A new test checks whether voice AI works well with real people
summary: Hume AI has published Real World VoiceEQ, a benchmark covering more than 40 voice models across 15-plus evaluation dimensions and 60-plus metrics. Built from more than one million human ratings, it measures qualities that transcripts miss, including emotion, speaker identity, noise robustness and conversational response.
friendlySummary: Real World VoiceEQ compares more than 40 voice AI systems. It tests tone, emotion, accents, noise and how naturally a system responds—not only whether it hears the words.
whyItMatters: Voice is becoming a common route into assistants, support and learning tools, but word accuracy alone does not ensure a good interaction. A broader public benchmark can help teams choose systems that listen, respond and remain reliable in real conditions.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Better tests can help people choose voice AI that is more helpful and less confusing in daily conversations.
